title: The Golden Eggs
content:
A golden Egg, one every day,
    That simpleton's Goose used to lay;
    So he killed the poor thing,
    Swifter fortune to bring,
    And dined off his fortune that day.

    Greed Overeaches Itself
meaning:
Walter Crane's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Golden Eggs"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
